privette v. superior court, 5 cal.4th 68.holding: Privette generally limits hirer liability for injuries to contractor employees when the injury arises from contracted work, but recognizes doctrinal limits and later exceptions.
privette v. superior court, 5 cal.4th 68.excerpt: A hirer is presumptively not liable to an independent contractor's employee for injuries arising from the contracted work.

seabright insurance co. v. us airways, i.holding: Seabright discusses delegation of workplace safety duties to independent contractors and limits tort claims against the hirer in that context.
seabright insurance co. v. us airways, i.excerpt: The hirer delegated workplace safety obligations to the contractor under the Privette line of decisions.

whitmore v. delgado scaffolding co., 334.holding: Federal district analysis applied contractor-control principles in a scaffolding injury setting and granted summary judgment where subcontractor controlled work method.
whitmore v. delgado scaffolding co., 334.excerpt: Summary judgment granted where subcontractor controlled scaffolding operations and risk allocation favored the hirer.

kellerman v. pacific coast construction,.holding: Compliance evidence may be relevant to reasonable care, but does not automatically eliminate all negligence disputes in every context.
kellerman v. pacific coast construction,.excerpt: Regulatory compliance is probative, not always dispositive, on standard-of-care issues.

torres v. granite falls dev. corp., 198.holding: California appellate decision addressing contractor-site liability boundaries under delegated safety duties.
torres v. granite falls dev. corp., 198.excerpt: Liability analysis turns on retained control and affirmative contribution factors.

blackwell v. sunrise contractors, inc.,.holding: Appellate treatment of contractor responsibilities and negligence allocation in construction-site injuries.
blackwell v. sunrise contractors, inc.,.excerpt: Control and duty allocation are central to negligence analysis.

nguyen v. allied pacific construction co.holding: Discusses workplace safety duties and limits of hirer liability in contractor arrangements.
nguyen v. allied pacific construction co.excerpt: Delegated safety obligations can narrow direct hirer exposure absent retained-control contribution.

reeves v. summit engineering group, 78 c.holding: Construction injury liability depends on control, duty assumptions, and factual contribution to the hazard.
reeves v. summit engineering group, 78 c.excerpt: Record-specific evidence controls whether hirer liability survives summary judgment.

dixon v. lone star structural, llc, 387.holding: Texas appellate authority on contractor and premises-liability allocation in industrial injury disputes.
dixon v. lone star structural, llc, 387.excerpt: Duty and control analysis limits owner or general-contractor exposure absent direct negligence.

okafor v. brightline builders, inc., 291.holding: Florida appellate decision addressing construction injury claims and the role of contractual duty delegation.
okafor v. brightline builders, inc., 291.excerpt: Delegation and control evidence are decisive in determining liability scope.
